description: Optimize the core RAG pipeline, deduplication, and search workflows based on comprehensive codebase analysis and 2025-2026 industry best practices. Focus areas: chunking quality, dedup performance, similar paper matching, image/multimodal processing, and workflow parallelization.

userStories.title: Replace character-based chunk sizing with token-based sizing
userStories.description: As a researcher, I need my paper chunks to be sized by tokens (not characters) so that embeddings are consistent across languages and the RAG pipeline performs optimally. Currently chunk_size=1024 counts characters via len(), but token_count uses len(text.split()) (word count) — these metrics are inconsistent and unreliable for CJK languages.
acceptanceCriteria: Use tiktoken (cl100k_base) for accurate token counting in all chunking functions
Default chunk_size is 512 tokens (not 1024 characters)
Default overlap is 50 tokens (not 100 words)
Token count in chunk metadata accurately reflects tiktoken count
chunk_mineru_markdown() uses token-based splitting
chunk_text() uses token-based splitting
marker-pdf fallback splits at token boundaries, not character boundaries
Test: verify a Chinese paragraph chunk has correct token count
Test: verify a 512-token English chunk has ~400-600 words (varies by tokenizer)
Test: verify overlap preserves last N tokens (not words) from previous chunk
userStories.notes: Files: backend/app/services/ocr_service.py lines 329-544. Add tiktoken dependency to backend requirements.py. Change chunk_size default from 1024 to 512 tokens. Replace len(text) with len(enc.encode(text)) for size checks. Replace len(text.split()) with len(enc.encode(text)) for token_count. Overlap: instead of word-based, encode, take last N tokens, decode.

userStories.title: Add sentence-aware paragraph splitting to chunking
userStories.description: As a researcher, I need chunks to respect sentence boundaries so that RAG retrieval returns complete thoughts, not mid-sentence fragments. Currently _flush_text_chunk() splits on paragraph boundaries only — long paragraphs are never subdivided, creating oversized chunks.
acceptanceCriteria: _flush_text_chunk() splits long paragraphs at sentence boundaries (sentence-ending punctuation: .。!?;)
No chunk exceeds token limit (512) even for very long paragraphs
Sentence splitting preserves the full sentence — never cuts mid-sentence
Fallback path (chunk_text) also respects sentence boundaries
Test: verify a 2000-character paragraph is split into multiple chunks at sentence boundaries
Test: verify each split chunk starts and ends with complete sentences
Test: verify overlap includes full sentences at the boundary
userStories.notes: Add sentence splitting regex: r'(?<=[.。!?])\s+' for English/Chinese. In _flush_text_chunk, if a single paragraph exceeds chunk_size, split it into sentences first, then accumulate sentences into chunks. This is more natural than word-based splitting.

userStories.title: Optimize dedup from O(N^2) to O(N) with token fingerprint pre-grouping
userStories.description: As a researcher with 1000+ papers, I need deduplication to complete in seconds, not minutes. Currently title_similarity_dedup and find_llm_dedup_candidates do O(N^2) pairwise SequenceMatcher comparisons — for 1000 papers, that is ~500K comparisons.
acceptanceCriteria: title_similarity_dedup uses token-based fingerprint pre-grouping to reduce comparisons
find_llm_dedup_candidates uses the same fingerprint optimization
For 1000 papers, comparisons drop from ~500K to ~10K or fewer
Dedup results are identical to before (same duplicates found, same papers removed)
Performance improvement is measurable (timing logs added)
Test: verify dedup finds same duplicates on a project with known duplicates
Test: verify 1000 papers complete in under 5 seconds
Test: verify edge cases like 'BERT (Pre-training...)' normalize correctly
userStories.notes: Files: backend/app/services/dedup_service.py. Create title_fingerprint() function: normalize, remove stop words, sort tokens, create frozenset hash. Group papers by fingerprint using defaultdict. Only run SequenceMatcher within fingerprint groups. Remove stop words set: {a, an, the, of, for, on, in, and, or, with, by, to, from, its, their}. Add timing: logger.info('Title dedup: %d papers, %d comparisons, %.2fs', n, comparisons, elapsed).

userStories.title: Eliminate PaddleOCR disk I/O by using in-memory image processing
userStories.description: As a researcher processing scanned PDFs, I need OCR to be fast. Currently PaddleOCR writes each page as a PNG to /tmp/ then reads it back — unnecessary disk I/O. PaddleOCR accepts PIL Image objects or numpy arrays directly.
acceptanceCriteria: extract_text_ocr() passes PIL Image to PaddleOCR directly without writing to /tmp
No temporary PNG files created during OCR
OCR results are identical to before
OCR speed improves measurably for multi-page scanned PDFs
Test: verify a scanned PDF is OCR'd correctly without temp files
Test: verify /tmp has no leftover OCR files after processing
Test: verify OCR output matches previous implementation
userStories.notes: Files: backend/app/services/ocr_service.py lines 156-203. Replace: pix = page.get_pixmap(dpi=150); pix.save('/tmp/page_N.png'); ocr_result = ocr.ocr('/tmp/page_N.png') with: pix = page.get_pixmap(dpi=150); img = Image.frombytes('RGB', [pix.width, pix.height], pix.samples); ocr_result = ocr.ocr(img). Remove all os.remove(temp_file) calls.

userStories.title: Enable MinerU image extraction and store figure metadata
userStories.description: As a researcher, I need figures from papers to be extracted and indexed. Currently MinerU's return_images is hardcoded to 'false', so all figure/image content is discarded. Figures are important context in scientific papers.
acceptanceCriteria: MinerU client sets return_images='true' in API payload
Extracted images are saved to project's figure storage directory
Figure paths are stored in chunk metadata (figure_path field already exists)
Figure chunks include image file reference in chunk metadata
Test: verify a PDF processed with MinerU has figure files saved
Test: verify figure_path in chunk metadata points to a valid file
Test: verify the image files are readable PNGs
userStories.notes: Files: backend/app/services/mineru_client.py line 63. Change 'return_images': 'false' to 'return_images': 'true'. After MinerU returns, save images from the response to /data0/djx/omelette/figures/{project_id}/paper_{paper_id}/. Update chunk metadata with correct figure_path. Create figures directory if needed.

userStories.title: Parallelize LangGraph OCR node with asyncio.gather
userStories.description: As a researcher uploading multiple PDFs, I want the LangGraph pipeline to process papers in parallel. Currently ocr_node in nodes.py processes papers sequentially with a for loop, while the background processor (paper_processor.py) already does parallel OCR with asyncio.gather.
acceptanceCriteria: ocr_node processes multiple papers concurrently using asyncio.gather
GPU semaphore limits parallelism to available GPUs
OCR results are identical to sequential processing
Processing time for N papers scales as O(N/GPU_COUNT) instead of O(N)
Error handling: if one paper fails, others continue
Test: verify 3 PDFs process in parallel (total time < sum of individual times)
Test: verify results match sequential processing output
Test: verify a single PDF failure doesn't crash the entire batch
userStories.notes: Files: backend/app/pipelines/nodes.py lines 294-330. Replace: for paper in papers: result = await ocr_service.process(paper.pdf_path) with: async def process_one(paper): async with OCRService() as ocr: return await ocr.process(paper.pdf_path); results = await asyncio.gather(*[process_one(p) for p in papers], return_exceptions=True). Use GPU semaphore from config (gpu_count).

userStories.title: Batch DOI check for search import instead of N separate queries
userStories.description: As a researcher running discovery searches, I need search import to be fast. Currently when auto_importing search results, the system does N separate DB queries to check if each DOI exists. This should be a single batch query.
acceptanceCriteria: Search import fetches all existing DOIs in a single SQL query
Results are filtered in Python to identify new papers
Import behavior is identical (same papers added, same skipped)
Test: verify a search with 50 results checks DOIs in 1 query instead of 50
Test: verify import results match before (same papers added/skipped)
userStories.notes: Files: backend/app/api/v1/search.py lines 58-73. Replace per-result DOI check with: existing_dois = set((await db.execute(select(Paper.doi).where(Paper.doi.in_(all_dois)))).scalars().all()). Then filter: new_papers = [p for p in results if p.doi not in existing_dois].

userStories.title: Add weighted chunk averaging for similar paper matching
userStories.description: As a researcher, I need similar paper recommendations to be more accurate. Currently all chunks are averaged equally, giving references section the same weight as abstract and conclusion. Important sections should contribute more to the paper's similarity vector.
acceptanceCriteria: Similar paper endpoint uses weighted averaging: abstract=3x, intro=2x, conclusion=2.5x, methods=1x, results=1.5x, discussion=2x, references=0x
Chunks without section metadata get default weight 1x
Median distance used instead of mean for robustness against outlier chunks
Similar papers ranking quality improves measurably
Test: verify abstract-heavy papers rank higher for similar-paper queries
Test: verify reference section content doesn't affect similarity scores
Test: verify API response format is unchanged
userStories.notes: Files: backend/app/api/v1/papers.py lines 362-456. Add SECTION_WEIGHTS dict. Modify chunk aggregation: instead of paper_scores[pid].append(dist), store (dist, section) pairs. Compute weighted mean distance. Replace sum(dists)/len(dists) with statistics.median(dists). Fetch section info from chunk metadata in ChromaDB.

userStories.title: Add cross-source deduplication to federated search results
userStories.description: As a researcher running discovery searches, I need the same paper to appear only once in results. Currently the federated search concatenates results from Semantic Scholar, OpenAlex, arXiv, and Crossref without any cross-source dedup — the same paper can appear 3-4 times.
acceptanceCriteria: Search results are deduplicated across providers using DOI matching
Papers without DOI are deduplicated using normalized title matching
When duplicates found, the version with most complete metadata is kept
Search API response includes a 'duplicates_removed' count
Test: verify a known paper found by 3 providers appears only once
Test: verify duplicates_removed count is accurate
Test: verify no papers are incorrectly deduplicated
userStories.notes: Files: backend/app/services/search_service.py. Add _dedup_results() method after gather. Group by DOI first (exact match). Then normalize titles and use fingerprint matching for no-DOI papers. Keep the version with most fields populated. Return merged results + dedup count.

userStories.title: Add PDF content hash for upload-time deduplication
userStories.description: As a researcher, I need to prevent uploading the exact same PDF twice with different filenames. Currently PDFs are saved with UUID-prefixed names and no content dedup check exists.
acceptanceCriteria: Upload endpoint computes SHA-256 hash of PDF content
Hash is stored in a new content_hash column on Paper model
Upload checks for existing papers with same content_hash before processing
Duplicate upload is rejected with a clear error message
Alembic migration adds content_hash column
Test: verify uploading the same PDF twice returns a conflict error
Test: verify different PDFs with same metadata are allowed
Test: verify content_hash is stored correctly in DB
userStories.notes: Files: backend/app/models/paper.py (add content_hash column), backend/app/api/v1/upload.py (compute hash before save), backend/alembic/versions/ (new migration). Use hashlib.sha256 on the file content before saving. Add UniqueConstraint on content_hash.
